text.title: MCP Basics: Model Context Protocol Server Development Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Author scaffolds 8 tools on day 1; debugging the SDK + transport + 8 schemas at once is overwhelming; project shelved.
antipattern.fix: Ship one tool. Add more after a week of real usage.

antipattern.symptom: Tool accepts free-text `payload` field. Agent passes garbage; debugging is impossible.
antipattern.root-cause: Author thought schema was a polish concern.
antipattern.fix: Mandatory Zod / Pydantic schema even for the first tool.

antipattern.symptom: Team can't figure out how to install the server. Author has to share Slack snippets.
antipattern.root-cause: Readme written from the developer's perspective, not the consumer's.
antipattern.fix: Top of README: 3-line install block with copy-paste `claude mcp add ...`.

antipattern.symptom: Server doesn't register at the gateway because manifest field name was wrong. Users hit it before the author did.
antipattern.fix: Mandatory `mcp-inspector` pass before any push to shared repo.
